response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with 15+ years across web, mobile, and backend systems, but their profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Their career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with only a brief 7-month stint (2014–2015) writing C/C++ components at National Instruments. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise, message-broker and MQTT-based industrial IoT development, and IEC 62443 SDL experience — none of which are evidenced. The DLang low-latency, high-throughput work at Sociomantic demonstrates transferable systems-programming skill, but it does not offset the missing C++ depth, industrial-automation domain, and security-standards background.
strengths: Strong systems-programming fundamentals from low-latency, async I/O, high-throughput DLang microservices work
Broad full-stack and event-driven architecture experience, including a self-founded geo-data platform on Kubernetes
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing (workshops, code katas, testing courses) aligned with the mentoring responsibility
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — a single ~7-month role over a decade ago, with no STL/modern C++ evidence since
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 / SDL security-standards exposure
Recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node product and AI tooling work) points away from embedded/industrial C++ development

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 experience
Transferable low-latency systems and multithreading experience (DLang)
